name: mc_regime_match_quality_insufficient_pool
description: Insufficient-eligible-pool fail-safe fixture. When the eligible candidate pool is below the MC sufficiency floor (eligible_days < MC_MIN_HISTORY_DAYS) the regime-match-quality assessment cannot be computed. compute_regime_match_quality MUST return an assessment whose mean_sq_mahalanobis is None AND is_unprecedented is False. is_unprecedented=False is the correct fail-safe: the EXISTING MC sentinel already short-circuits run_monte_carlo's prob_beating to None on this branch (project memory project_mc_eligible_pool_vs_raw_day_boundary), and compute_exit_confirmation treats None as 'MC unavailable' so the trailing stop fires on its own condition. An is_unprecedented=True on this branch would be a redundant second sentinel signalling the same path and would risk double-counting in telemetry.
provenance: Schema-derived synthetic history. Day count is set BELOW the eligible-pool boundary (MC_MIN_HISTORY_DAYS + MC_VOL_WINDOW_DAYS - 1 = 39 raw days) so the sufficiency check fails. No producer-computed score is pinned.
derivation: 10 raw days is well below the eligible-pool boundary of MC_MIN_HISTORY_DAYS + MC_VOL_WINDOW_DAYS - 1 = 39 raw days. compute_regime_match_quality returns the insufficient sentinel on its score and is_unprecedented=False; telemetry should record a human-readable insufficient_reason.
